rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with their expression types (int, real, bool).
Remove invalid guarantee or equation clauses that reference undefined symbols or have type errors.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always reference visible AADL features or previously declared helpers.
Ensure all AGREE expressions have consistent and resolvable types to prevent <error> type issues.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in constant and equation declarations.
When diagnostics report type mismatches, correct the declaration to match the expression's actual type.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings belong in implementation scopes, not component types.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared variables.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only equations or constants.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not declared in the model or its dependencies.
Do not add new architecture elements to resolve AGREE diagnostics; only fix existing references or remove invalid clauses.
Avoid placing invalid or unresolved identifiers in AGREE equations or guarantees.
Do not assume that AGREE will infer types from context; explicitly declare correct types.
irrelevant_topics: Component type annexes vs implementation annexes
